summary: Architecture context scan for local topic title keyword and node blocking.

findings.evidence: servicies/types.ts defines Topic with id, title, member, node and reply_count. Topic.node?.name is available for node matching.
servicies/node.ts defines k.node.all, fetching /api/nodes/all.json as Promise<Node[]>.
screens/SearchNodeScreen.tsx filters k.node.all by title, title_alternative, name and aliases. It is currently single-select through onPressNodeItem.
screens/SortTabsScreen.tsx navigates to SearchNode and adds a selected node as a home tab.
screens/SettingScreen.tsx uses ListItem entries for settings navigation and already places Theme Settings and Black List near the desired location.
HomeScreen, NodeTopicsScreen, MyTopicsScreen, MyFollowingScreen, MemberDetailScreen, HotestTopicsScreen and SearchScreen render Topic-like lists.
jotai/utils/atomWithAsyncStorage.ts wraps AsyncStorage with jotai atomWithStorage and is used by existing preference atoms.
package.json has no dedicated bottom sheet library. A small local React Native Modal-based sheet avoids adding a dependency.

risks: Route params already carry callbacks in SearchNode; keep compatibility and avoid widening non-serializable usage beyond existing pattern.
FlatList ListHeaderComponent varies by page; blocked notice must compose with existing headers.
Search results are Topic-like rather than direct Topic instances and need mapping before filtering.
Browser Web support depends on the existing local Expo Web dev setup.
